sourceMovie.title: The Dig
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Dig for the same reason it broke through in 2021: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a history rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Simon Stone keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Darkest Hour
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Dig clicked for you, Darkest Hour (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Dig?
faq.answer: Try Darkest Hour, Suffragette, The Guernsey Literary & Potato Peel Pie Society—each shares DNA with The Dig while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Dig?
faq.answer: The Dig (2021) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Dig worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
